preamble: Prothesis of [i] is common in early stages of Western Romance and in languages like Turkish when adapting foreign loanwords with initial consonant clusters.

examples.note: Turkish lacks initial consonant clusters; prothetic [i] inserted before foreign [st-] in loanword adaptation.
Prothetic [i] (hamzat al-wasl) before initial consonant clusters; e.g., 'ibn' (son) → [ʔibn]. Abstract pattern; specific examples not cited.

sources: Lewis, G. L. (1967). Turkish Grammar. Clarendon Press.
